name: English Cheese Pie
recipeInstructions:
CRUST: Sift together the flour, salt, and sugar into a medium-sized mixing bowl. Add the butter to the flour all at once. Mix together until well blended. Then add the shortening and continue to blend by cutting it into the flour mixture. The mixture will begin to look like crumbs or small pebbles. It will have the texture of oatmeal. Sprinkle the water over the dough, distributing it evenly throughout. Gather the dough into a ball and wrap in plastic wrap. Chill for at least 30 minutes. Preheat the oven to 450°F. When the pastry has chilled, roll out on a pastry board to a thickness of 1/8-inch. Roll the dough between two sheets of waxed paper if needed. Transfer to a 9-inch pie tin and gently press into the bottom without stretching it. Use excess pastry for flutes or cut away with a sharp knife or scissors. Prick the bottom of the pastry shell thoroughly to prevent trapped air from bubbling the dough. Butter a sheet of aluminum foil, place it in the shell, and weight it down with weights or beans. Bake the crust for 7 to 10 minutes, then remove the weight and foil. Return the crust to the oven and continue baking until lightly browned.
FILLING: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Press cottage cheese through a sieve. In a small saucepan, gently heat cream and sherry until steaming; do not allow it to scorch. In a mixing bowl, beat together eggs, egg yolks, sugar, rosewater, and spices until frothy (about 5 minutes). Beat the cottage cheese smooth, then add egg mixture and blend well. Add hot cream and sherry slowly to the cheese mixture and beat until well blended. Stir in currants and pour into prepared crust. Bake for about 30 minutes, then allow to cool. Chill and sprinkle with cinnamon.
